title: OpenDocument Presentation
documentation:
## Purpose
Carries OpenDocument Presentation files (`.odp`), the ISO-standardized slide format used by LibreOffice Impress and Apache OpenOffice Impress. ODP is a ZIP archive of XML content following the ODF specification.

## When to use
Use `media:odp` when a pipeline node processes open-format presentations — for example, a slide rendering step via LibreOffice, a content extraction step, or a conversion step targeting PDF or PPTX output. For Microsoft PowerPoint format, see [PowerPoint Presentation](https://capdag.com/browse/media:pptx).

## Notes
ODP follows the OASIS OpenDocument Format standard (ISO/IEC 26300). LibreOffice Impress provides the most complete support, including complex animations and transitions. Conversion to PDF for archival or distribution is a common pipeline operation. See also [OpenDocument Presentation](https://capdag.com/browse/media:odp).
